quotes.text: The best time to set up CI/CD was when you started the project. The second best time is now. The worst time is after your third 'it works on my machine' incident.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Deploy on Friday if you want. Just don't deploy on Friday afternoon and then leave.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best alerting system is one that pages you about things that actually matter. The worst is one that pages you about everything so you ignore it all.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your staging environment should be a copy of production. If it's not, it's a lie you tell yourself.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Kubernetes is what happens when sysadmins get bored and decide to turn 'turn it off and on again' into a distributed system.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Docker: because 'it works on my machine' stopped being acceptable. Now it works on everyone's machine but nobody understands why.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you need a PhD to understand your deployment pipeline, you've built a research project, not a product.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Monitoring tells you what's broken. Observability tells you why. Most teams have neither.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best incident response is the one where you don't need to respond because the system healed itself. The second best is one with a runbook.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Post-mortems are like therapy: everyone feels better afterward, but nothing actually changes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every production incident is a lesson. If you're not learning from them, you're just collecting them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you can't describe your deployment process in 3 steps, it's too complicated.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your CI pipeline is your safety net. If it's slow, developers skip it. If they skip it, you're shipping bugs.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your logging should tell a story. If your logs are noise, your monitoring is useless.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your CI pipeline should be faster than your coffee break. If it's not, optimize it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best on-call rotation is the one that never fires. The second best is the one that fires at reasonable hours.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your deployment process requires a runbook, it's too complicated. Simplify it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add health checks is before you need them. Not during an incident.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Rollback is not a failure. Rollback is a feature. Use it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best on-call engineers are the ones who fix the problem AND the alert. Not just the problem.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every production incident should result in exactly two things: a fix and a better alert.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best monitoring tells you what's broken. The best observability tells you why. Most teams have neither.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your SLA should be honest. If you can't hit 99.9%, don't promise it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best incident response is one where you don't panic. Panic is the enemy of resolution.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your staging environment should match production. If it doesn't, your staging is lying to you.
quotes.author: based
